title: Energy spectra and cascade in the spin turbulence of a driven spinor Bose-Einstein condensate
authors: Junghoon Lee, Jongmin Kim, Donggyu Lee, Yong-il Shin
abstract: We investigate the spin-interaction energy spectrum of spin turbulence in a driven spinor Bose-Einstein condensate. Continuous spin driving of a spin-1 condensate produces a nonequilibrium steady state with spatially fluctuating magnetization. We observe a power-law scaling consistent with the $-7/3$ exponent predicted for spin-wave turbulence, which persists across our full range of drive strengths despite substantial changes in the spectral anisotropy. After switching off the drive, we track the free-decay evolution and find evidence consistent with a direct cascade of spin-interaction energy toward higher wavenumbers. These results establish an energy-spectral hallmark of spin turbulence and enable quantitative studies of cascade dynamics in spinor superfluids.
summary: This paper experimentally investigates spin turbulence in a driven spinor Bose-Einstein condensate. By measuring the spin-interaction energy spectrum, the authors confirm a universal power-law scaling of $k^{-7/3}$, characteristic of energy cascades. These results establish a quantitative tool for studying energy transport in spinor superfluids.
main_problem: The study investigates the energy spectrum and cascade dynamics associated with spin turbulence in a driven spinor Bose-Einstein condensate.
main_result: The experiment confirms a robust energy-spectral hallmark of spin turbulence, observing a power-law scaling exponent consistent with $k^{-7/3}$ across various drive strengths.
method: The research uses spin-selective imaging of a spinor BEC to reconstruct magnetization densities, followed by 2D Fourier analysis to extract the energy spectrum $E_s(\mathbf{k})$.
model_or_system: The physical system is a spin-1 Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC) of $^{23}	ext{Na}$ atoms, which is driven by a continuous resonant RF magnetic field ($\Omega$) to induce a non-equilibrium steady state of spin turbulence.
key_observables: Spin-interaction energy spectrum $E_s(\mathbf{k})$, spectral anisotropy ($\eta_s$), and the power-law scaling exponent $ eta$.
important_parameters: Driving strength ($\Omega$), quadratic Zeeman shift ($q$), and the characteristic exponent $-7/3$.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on correcting the measured power spectrum using the Modulation Transfer Function (MTF) and assumes negligible density redistribution during the short imaging delay.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the dependence of the energy spectrum $E_s(\mathbf{k})$ on driving strength $\Omega$, demonstrating the universal $k^{-7/3}$ scaling, and show the time evolution of energy components during free decay.
paper_structure: The paper details the experimental setup and measurement techniques, analyzes the steady-state energy spectrum under continuous driving, examines the spectral anisotropy as a function of drive strength, and finally tracks the energy decay dynamics after switching off the drive to confirm the energy cascade.
why_interesting: This work provides direct experimental evidence of universal energy cascade scaling laws (like $k^{-7/3}$) in a controllable quantum many-body system, offering a clean platform to test theoretical models of nonlinear wave turbulence.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Spin turbulence in a trapped spinor Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC). (a) Schematic of the experimental setup. A BEC of F = 1 23Na atoms was prepared in an optical dipole trap (ODT) under a uniform magnetic field along z and continuously driven by a transverse resonant RF mag- netic field with Rabi frequency Ω. (b) Representative image of the effective areal magnetization M2D,z in a steady turbulent BEC. The rectangular box indicates the region selected for de- termining the spin-interaction energy spectrum. (c,d) Energy spectra at two different driving strengths of (c) Ω/2π = 9.5 Hz and (d) 50 Hz. The colored lines show the directional energy spectra Ei(k) (i = x, y, z) and the black...

FIG. 2. Spin-interaction energy spectra in spin turbulence. (a) Energy spectra Es(k) at various driving strengths Ω. The vertical dotted line indicates ¯kℓ, the mean lower bound of the high-k scaling range across all Ωvalues [38]. (b) Rescaled energy spectra Es(k)/Ap. The prefactor Ap was extracted by fitting Es(k)/(Es0/ks) = Ap(k/ks)−7/3 to each spectrum in the range k ≥1.3 ¯kℓ. The inset shows Ap as a function of Ω(see Supplemental Material [38] for a model describing the energy injection rate). FIG. 3. Free decay of spin turbulence. (a) Time evolution of the quadratic Zeeman energy Eq (gray squares), total spin- interaction energy Es (gray circles), and its transeverse (E⊥= Ex + Ey, purple), and longitudinal (Ez, green) components. (b) Transverse spectra E⊥(k) = Ex(k)+Ey(k) for hold times t = 0, 10, 50, 200, 400-ms after switching off the drive. The inset shows the corresponding longitudinal spectra Ez(k). Red solid lines indicate the k−7/3 power law, and black dashed lines denote the spectra at t = 0.
